module1.clean_text: The patient is a [AGE] pregnant woman, gravida 4, para 1, with a history of two abortions in the first and second trimesters. Her first pregnancy ended in a spontaneous first-trimester miscarriage. In [DATE] her second pregnancy was complicated by oligohydramnios, and she underwent a planned cesarean section in the third trimester, delivering a healthy male baby three weeks before the expected date. In [DATE] her third pregnancy was terminated at 16 weeks, followed by surgical evacuation after a failed induction. Further details about this pregnancy are unavailable. The patient presented to the emergency department with shortness of breath that had progressively worsened over a few days and was exacerbated by moderate exertion. She was 16 weeks pregnant at the time, despite previous advice against pregnancy. She reported no chest pain, productive cough, fever, hemoptysis, dizziness, abdominal pain, vaginal bleeding, or other cardiorespiratory or genitourinary symptoms. Her medications included aspirin 100 mg once daily and folic acid 5 mg daily. On examination, she was afebrile with a blood pressure of 112/74 mmHg and a pulse rate of 101 bpm. Her chest was clear with normal first and second heart sounds, and a pansystolic murmur radiating to the carotids was noted. Laboratory investigations revealed normal cardiac markers, creatinine, electrolyte and lipid profiles, and normal liver and thyroid function tests. Her complete blood count showed mild leukocytosis (white blood cell count of 13.30 × 10^3/uL), iron-deficiency anemia with a hemoglobin level of 11.0 g/dL, and a normal platelet count. NT-proBNP was mildly elevated at 547.2 pg/mL. An electrocardiogram indicated sinus tachycardia and left ventricular hypertrophy with a strain pattern. A chest X-ray was normal.

phenopacket.input: The patient is a 35-year-old pregnant woman, gravida 4, para 1, with a history of two abortions in the first and second trimesters. Her first pregnancy ended in a spontaneous first-trimester miscarriage. In 2013, her second pregnancy was complicated by oligohydramnios, and she underwent a planned cesarean section in the third trimester, delivering a healthy male baby three weeks before the expected date. In 2015, her third pregnancy was terminated at 16 weeks, followed by surgical evacuation after a failed induction. Further details about this pregnancy are unavailable. The patient presented to the emergency department with shortness of breath that had progressively worsened over a few days and was exacerbated by moderate exertion. She was 16 weeks pregnant at the time, despite previous advice against pregnancy. She reported no chest pain, productive cough, fever, hemoptysis, dizziness, abdominal pain, vaginal bleeding, or other cardiorespiratory or genitourinary symptoms. Her medications included aspirin 100 mg once daily and folic acid 5 mg daily. On examination, she was afebrile with a blood pressure of 112/74 mmHg and a pulse rate of 101 bpm. Her chest was clear with normal first and second heart sounds, and a pansystolic murmur radiating to the carotids was noted. Laboratory investigations revealed normal cardiac markers, creatinine, electrolyte and lipid profiles, and normal liver and thyroid function tests. Her complete blood count showed mild leukocytosis (white blood cell count of 13.30 × 10^3/uL), iron-deficiency anemia with a hemoglobin level of 11.0 g/dL, and a normal platelet count. NT-proBNP was mildly elevated at 547.2 pg/mL. An electrocardiogram indicated sinus tachycardia and left ventricular hypertrophy with a strain pattern. A chest X-ray was normal.
